reviews.title: Silicone is is good. But key ring won't last
reviews.text: The silicone part was easy to put on. The adhesion holds well. But my bud case did open and a bud fell out. The problem is the key ring. The decorative pieces fell off within a month. Then the lobster claw hook fell off a few weeks later. Luckily, it happened while I was at home and not out where I would have lost my ear buds.

reviews.title: Cheap doesn't fit properly. Not a good design
reviews.text: Cheap doesn't fit properly. Not a good fit. Looks very cheap.

reviews.title: Does the job, looks cute
reviews.text: It definitely looks like the picture. The rubber is flimsy, and difficult to attach to the headphone case. It took me two tries to get it to fit the bottom appropriately, because the first time it slipped and I couldn't fit the charging cord through the back. But others might be better at this. The keychain is pretty. The clip to attach it is smaller than I anticipated and almost didn't fit my purse. It will protect the case from scratches, but that's hardly why I bought it- it's sparkly and pretty and does the job well enough.

reviews.title: Do not buy.
reviews.text: It broke one day later.

reviews.title: For my son’s gaming headset...
reviews.text: This little contraption is quite awesome. Bought it for the headset my son uses while playing video games. Was tired of finding his headset left on the floor, so attached this to his entertainment center and now he hangs his $100 headset on this (up off the floor where it won’t get stepped on/ damaged).  Solid construction. Easy install (no holes, no tools/ other hardware required).

reviews.title: Great purchase
reviews.text: It was just a tad bit too small for where I wanted it,  but I found the perfect spot on the printer stand next to the desk. Very well made.  Not just a cheap piece of plastic.  Strong and sturdy. Folds up out of the way when not in use.

reviews.title: This Gadget is Great!
reviews.text: Love this thing! Put it under my desk to hold my purse. How easy can it be?! Slide it on. Tighten it securely. Add purse. Done and done! Nice quality, too…not cheesy or flimsy.

reviews.title: Great and well made
reviews.text: This thing is great and well made from metal.  This is my second one.  It cost more than the plastics ones, but it is very well made.  The holder folds up once you have it clamped to a desk to shelf.  It is strong and supports heavy headphones well.  It is a little expensive but well worth the investment.

reviews.title: Nice, just don't like having to remove them to charge it
reviews.text: I really like them and my AirPods feel much more secure in my ears.  I can move my head and they don't fall out.  The only thing I don't like is that they won't charge in the charger unless you remove them.

reviews.title: Solves AirPod Issue
reviews.text: I have a love/hate relationship with my AirPods and these are making me love them a LOT more. On their own, my AirPods cause my ears to hurt terribly. Not to mention, they fall out at the slightest movement, like smiling or turning my head. Talk about ANNOYING. These tips cure those ailments. Now I can exercise with my AirPods in and never worry about them falling out. I have tiny ears, and the fit is great with no discomfort at all. I love that the little hook sticks up into the cartilage of my ear to make a really secure fit. The silicon keeps the buds from irritating my ears too. I’m so happy I got these. Now I can actually put my AirPods to good use.

reviews.title: Good sound and battery life, touch activated button bad
reviews.text: Overall, this is a good earbud. However, if you hold it in your hand you run the risk of your skin/touch activating the voice commands, it hears a random snippet of what you’re saying and it calls someone from your address book that kinda sorta sounds like what you had said and you have to quickly try to hang up as your phone dials someone you haven’t talked to in 5 years, you whispering “oh f*** nononono” over and over until it hangs up and you pray it didn’t actually connect on their side but it does every damn time and then you’re forced into a conversation of pleasantries. So like...yeah it works well, good range but the touch activated button was a big no from me.

reviews.title: Not quite a perfect fit, but works well
reviews.text: Purchased for a 6-yr-old Sony MDR-ZX750BN. A tight fit to get the zipper closed, yet there is a bit of extra on the side that connects with my head. The cover is about a centimeter short of full coverage. That said, it is comfortable; and that is what is important. This should last at least another 6 years, making the little bit I paid well worth it.

reviews.title: Fits perfectly on ATH-M50. Nice stretchy neoprene means they fit a lot more headphones than what's listed.
reviews.text: My Audio-Technica ATH-M50 is still by far my favorite wired headphone, but I haven't been using them because of the cheap black vinyl on the M50 headband that is famous for peeling off and leaving flakes all over your head. This band fit perfectly. See attached pics.

reviews.title: Perfect for me

reviews.title: This headband cover was just what I needed!
reviews.text: The Geekria headband cover was purchased to replace the Sony piece that was deteriorating after a couple of years of usage.  This replacement headband cover is very comfortable. It was a little bit of a challenge to install (it goes directly over the old headband) but once on, it works great. I would buy it again.
